source.title: Parallel processing streams in the hippocampus

confidence_rationale: Review paper synthesizing electrophysiology and lesion evidence across multiple species. Directly describes a dedicated object-identity stream (LEC) that is anatomically and functionally separate from the spatial stream (MEC), paralleling the z_resource / z_world split proposed in SD-015. Confidence bounded by the fact that the LEC 'what' stream is not purely object-type identity -- it also carries egocentric relational and temporal information -- so the mapping is directional rather than isomorphic.
failure_signatures: If LEC neurons encode raw positional information as strongly as object identity, the 'what' stream argument weakens and the motivation for a separate z_resource encoder is reduced.
If hippocampal place cells downstream encode object type independently of location even without LEC input, then z_resource could be derived from z_world rather than requiring a separate encoder.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The hippocampus receives two anatomically and functionally distinct input streams: the lateral entorhinal cortex (LEC) conveying object-centered, non-spatial ('what') information and the medial entorhinal cortex (MEC) conveying allocentric spatial ('where') information. Dentate gyrus and CA3 bind these streams into configural object+place representations; CA1 integrates separated outputs from both streams.
mapping.ree_translation: z_resource (ResourceEncoder output) corresponds to the LEC what-stream: an object-type representation invariant to absolute spatial position. z_world (the spatial scene embedding) corresponds to the MEC where-stream. Both are inputs to the hippocampal module, which binds them into episodic object-place memories. SD-015's proposal that goal-directed approach requires a dedicated z_resource encoder rather than reading object type off z_world is directly supported: the brain does not derive object identity from its spatial map; it maintains a parallel dedicated channel.
mapping.mapping_caveat: LEC in rodents encodes egocentric relational information ('object near left wall') rather than pure categorical object-type features. This means LEC is not a clean 'what' stream in the IT cortex sense -- it blends identity with local relational geometry. The SD-015 ResourceEncoder is designed to extract abstract object-type features invariant to spatial position, which is a stronger invariance requirement than LEC typically satisfies. The mapping is directionally correct but the degree of spatial invariance differs.
mapping.source_context: Rodent electrophysiology and lesion studies; human fMRI confirmatory evidence cited. Virtual and real maze navigation tasks.

confidence_components.notes: High-quality review synthesizing a large evidence base. Mapping fidelity is good directionally but the LEC-to-z_resource analogy has a precision limit: LEC is not purely object-type invariant to location. Transfer risk is low because the parallel-streams architecture is conserved across mammalian species.
